etymology.description: semantic 肉(肉) (ròu, "flesh") + phonetic 退(退) (tuì) -> leg; thigh
etymology.interpretation: The character 腿(tuǐ, "leg; thigh") combines 肉(肉) (ròu, "flesh") for its semantic input pertaining to the body part with 退(退) (tuì) as the phonetic component, contributing the sound. The overall concept refers to the fleshy limb, specifically a leg or thigh, as something part of the body's flesh.

examples.chinese: 他摔倒后腿疼得厉害(他摔倒後腿疼得厲害)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shuāidǎo hòu tuǐ téng de lìhài.
examples.english: After he fell, his leg hurt terribly.

examples.chinese: 她的猫喜欢在沙发上伸腿(她的貓喜歡在沙發上伸腿)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de māo xǐhuān zài shāfā shàng shēn tuǐ.
examples.english: Her cat likes to stretch its legs on the sofa.

examples.chinese: 运动员需要强壮的腿来比赛(運動員需要強壯的腿來比賽)。
examples.pinyin: Yùndòngyuán xūyào qiángzhuàng de tuǐ lái bǐsài.
examples.english: Athletes need strong legs to compete.
